name: write_file_tool
summary: Create a new file or overwrite an existing one. Parent directories are created automatically.
Returns a one-line confirmation naming the path.
constraints: The path is resolved relative to the agent's working directory.
Writes the entire file content in one shot — there is no append mode.
An existing file at `path` is replaced; the previous content is gone after the call returns.
anti_patterns: To modify part of an existing file — use `edit_file_tool`. It only sends the diff and is much cheaper.
To read a file before deciding what to write — use `read_file_tool` first.
cautions: ALWAYS read an existing file with `read_file_tool` before overwriting it. Otherwise you are guessing what you are about to destroy.
IMPORTANT: prefer `edit_file_tool` for changes to existing files. Reach for `write_file_tool` only when creating a new file or doing a full rewrite.
IMPORTANT: overwriting an existing file is recoverable only from version control or an out-of-band backup.
output: Plain text. `File written: <path>` on success. On failure: an error message (parent-dir creation failed, write failed).

path.description: Path to the file to write, relative to the working directory. Parent directories are created automatically.

content.description: Full file content. Replaces any existing content at `path` byte-for-byte.
